# Seaside Paradise Restaurant

> Real Caribbean food in the heart of Bodden Town, Grand Cayman, Cayman Islands.

Seaside Paradise Restaurant is a waterfront Caribbean restaurant located at 238 Bodden Town Road, Bodden Town, Grand Cayman, KY2-2500, Cayman Islands. It is owned and operated by Rodneys Group Ltd and serves authentic Caymanian and Caribbean cuisine daily.

## Key Facts

- **Full name:** Seaside Paradise Restaurant
- **Address:** 238 Bodden Town Road, Bodden Town, Grand Cayman, KY2-2500, Cayman Islands
- **Phone:** +1 (345) 516-4367
- **Email:** seasideparadiseky@gmail.com
- **Hours:** Monday – Sunday, 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM (open every day of the year)
- **Cuisine:** Caribbean, Caymanian, Jamaican-influenced, Fresh Seafood
- **Price range:** $$ (moderate; mains $9–$30)
- **Setting:** Waterfront beach terrace, Bodden Town, Grand Cayman
- **Owner:** Rodneys Group Ltd
- **Social:** Instagram @seasideparadiserestaurant | TikTok @seasideparadisefood

## Menu Highlights

### Signature Dishes
- Slow-Braised Oxtail — $10–15. Braised for hours until fall-off-the-bone tender. Served with rice and peas. Most-ordered dish.
- Jerk Chicken — $9–12. Marinated overnight in scotch bonnet and allspice, cooked over open heat.
- Curry Goat — $10–14. Slow-cooked in Caribbean curry with potatoes and peppers.
- Brown Stew Fish — $16–30. Fresh local fish in rich brown gravy with island spices.
- Rondon — Traditional Caymanian salt fish slow-cooked in coconut milk with breadfruit and ground provisions.
- Cow Foot Soup — $5–10. Traditional Caribbean soup, a beloved local staple.

### Fish & Seafood
- Fry Fish, Steam Fish, Brown Stew Fish — $16–30
- Pepper Shrimp, Garlic Shrimp, Curry Shrimp, Coconut Shrimp — $15 each

### Chicken
- Fry Chicken, Baked Chicken, BBQ Chicken, Jerk Chicken — $6–12

### Sides
- Festival (x3) — $2
- Steam Bami — $5
- Steam Veg, Stir Fry Veg — $5

### Natural Drinks
- Pine & Ginger — $6
- Strawberry Mango — $6
- Soursop with Lime — $6
- Peanut Punch (Strong Back) — $7

## About the Restaurant

Seaside Paradise Restaurant is a beloved local institution in Bodden Town, Grand Cayman. The restaurant cooks fresh every day — the menu follows the catch and what local fishermen and farmers bring in that morning. No shortcuts, no frozen ingredients. Just real Caribbean cooking.

The restaurant sits on a waterfront beach terrace overlooking the ocean, where guests can enjoy the sea breeze, feed the tarpon, and experience authentic Caymanian hospitality.

Seaside Paradise is part of Rodneys Group Ltd and has been serving the Bodden Town community for years.

## Catering & Private Events

Seaside Paradise caters events across Grand Cayman and hosts private events at the restaurant. Contact +1 (345) 516-4367 to discuss catering requirements.

## Location Context

Bodden Town is the oldest settlement on Grand Cayman, located on the south coast of the island. Seaside Paradise Restaurant is on Bodden Town Road, the main road through the town, with direct waterfront access.
